object-cover object-top"}],["$","div",null,{"children":[["$","p",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-stone-700 dark:text-stone-200 text-sm group-hover:text-orange-600 transition-colors","children":"James Park"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xs text-stone-400","children":"Tech Reviewer"}]]}]]}],["$","span",null,{"className":"flex items-center gap-1","children":[["$","$L6",null,{"ref":"$undefined","iconNode":[["circle",{"cx":"12","cy":"12","r":"10","key":"1mglay"}],["path",{"d":"M12 6v6l4 2","key":"mmk7yg"}]],"className":"lucide-clock","size":13}]," ","8 min read"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"April 25, 2026"}]]}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-10 pb-16","children":[["$","article",null,{"className":"lg:col-span-2","children":[["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xl text-stone-600 leading-relaxed mb-6 font-medium pl-4 border-l-4 border-orange-500","children":"What blue light glasses actually do, what they don't, and the five pairs worth buying — written by someone who read the research instead of the marketing copy."}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xs text-stone-500 dark:text-stone-400 mb-8 p-3 rounded-lg bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-900 border border-stone-200 dark:border-stone-800","children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Affiliate disclosure:"}]," This post may contain affiliate links to products we recommend. We earn a small commission when you buy through them, at no extra cost to you. As an Amazon Associate we earn from qualifying purchases. See our ",["$","$L7",null,{"href":"/disclaimer","className":"text-orange-600 dark:text-orange-400 hover:underline","children":"disclaimer"}]," for details."]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"prose","children":[["$","h2","0",{"id":"what-blue-light-glasses-actually-do-and-what-they-don-t","children":"What Blue Light Glasses Actually Do (and What They Don't)"}],["$","p","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Walk into any optical store and you'll see \"computer glasses\" or \"blue light glasses\" pitched as the cure for digital eye strain. The marketing has been so aggressive for so long that most people assume the science is settled. It isn't."}}],"$L29","$L2a","$L2b","$L2c","$L2d","$L2e","$L2f","$L30","$L31","$L32","$L33","$L34","$L35","$L36","$L37","$L38","$L39","$L3a","$L3b","$L3c","$L3d","$L3e","$L3f","$L40","$L41","$L42","$L43","$L44","$L45","$L46","$L47","$L48","$L49","$L4a","$L4b","$L4c","$L4d","$L4e","$L4f","$L50","$L51","$L52","$L53"]}],"$L54","$L55","$L56"]}],"$L57"]}]]}]
58:I[94097,["/_next/static/chunks/0wjbooevnfpw4.js","/_next/static/chunks/0wu484j-81s3l.js"],"default"]
59:I[24170,["/_next/static/chunks/0wjbooevnfpw4.js","/_next/static/chunks/0wu484j-81s3l.js"],"default"]
5a:I[55132,["/_next/static/chunks/0wjbooevnfpw4.js","/_next/static/chunks/0wu484j-81s3l.js"],"default"]
:HL["https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1511671782779-c97d3d27a1d4?w=1200&h=800&fit=crop&auto=format&q=80","image"]
:HL["https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1456406644174-8ddd4cd52a06?w=1200&h=800&fit=crop&auto=format&q=80","image"]
29:["$","p","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Here's what the research actually says:"}}]
2a:["$","ul","3",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Reduce eye strain?</strong> Multiple meta-analyses (most prominently a 2023 <em>Cochrane Database</em> review) found <strong>no significant evidence</strong> that blue-light-blocking glasses reduce eye strain compared to clear lenses. Eye strain comes from staring at one focal distance for hours, dry-eye from reduced blinking, and ambient lighting issues — not from the wavelength of light hitting your eye."}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Improve sleep?</strong> This is where the science is real. Blue light suppresses melatonin production, the hormone that signals your body to sleep. <strong>Wearing blue-light-blocking glasses 2–3 hours before bed</strong> can improve sleep latency and quality, especially for users who scroll phones in bed. Studies on shift workers and adolescents have shown measurable benefits."}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Prevent macular damage?</strong> No human evidence. Some lab studies on isolated cells suggest blue light may contribute to retinal stress, but no real-world study has shown blue-light glasses prevent any eye disease."}}]]}]
2b:["$","p","4",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Translation: <strong>buy them for sleep, not for eye comfort during the workday.</strong>"}}]
2c:["$","h2","5",{"id":"why-buy-them-anyway","children":"Why Buy Them Anyway"}]
2d:["$","p","6",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Despite the eye-strain marketing being shaky, blue light glasses still have a legitimate place in a home office:"}}]
2e:["$","ul","7",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Evening computer use</strong> — if you finish your workday after 7 PM and still face a screen, blue-light blocking around the ~470 nm wavelength helps your circadian rhythm."}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Late-night browsing</strong> — phones are the worst offenders for blue light exposure within 2 hours of bedtime."}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Combined with mild reading correction</strong> — many \"computer glasses\" double as low-power readers (+0.50, +0.75 diopters) that reduce focus strain on screens at typical desk distance."}}]]}]
2f:["$","p","8",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"If you're under 35 with no sleep issues and only use screens during daytime, you probably don't need blue light glasses at all. Better solutions to eye strain are covered in our <a href=\"/blog/reduce-eye-strain-working-from-home\" class=\"text-orange-600 dark:text-orange-400 hover:underline font-medium\">reduce eye strain working from home guide</a>."}}]
30:["$","h2","9",{"id":"what-to-look-for","children":"What to Look For"}]
31:["$","ul","10",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Blocking percentage</strong> — clear-lens glasses block 5–15% of blue light (better than nothing for evening). Yellow- or amber-tinted lenses block 50–98% (better for sleep, but distort screen color significantly)."}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Anti-reflective coating</strong> — reduces glare from monitors and overhead lights. Worth more than the blue-light filter for actual daytime comfort."}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Frame fit</strong> — like any glasses, fit matters more than the lens technology. Ill-fitting frames slide, pinch, or sit too far from the eyes to be useful."}}],["$","li","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Optional reading prescription</strong> — if you're 35+, a +0.50 to +1.00 diopter \"computer reading\" boost reduces focus strain and is genuinely helpful. Available without a prescription on most online glasses retailers."}}]]}]
32:["$","h2","11",{"id":"top-picks-for-2026","children":"Top Picks for 2026"}]
33:["$","h3","12",{"id":"1-felix-gray-faraday-best-overall","children":"1. Felix Gray Faraday — Best Overall"}]
34:["$","p","13",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Felix Gray nailed the design-frames-for-screen-workers niche. Faraday is their bestseller — clear lenses with subtle blue-blocking, anti-reflective coating, and frames that look like real glasses, not gamer accessories."}}]
35:["$","ul","14",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Blue light blocking:</strong> ~15% (clear lens, low distortion)"}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Lens features:</strong> Anti-reflective, anti-glare, scratch-resistant"}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Frame:</strong> Acetate, classic round/oval shapes"}}],["$","li","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Price:</strong> ~$95"}}]]}]
36:["$","p","15",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The design is the differentiator. They look like Warby Parker frames, not \"tech accessory.\""}}]
37:["$","h3","16",{"id":"2-gunnar-optiks-intercept-best-for-heavy-evening-use","children":"2. Gunnar Optiks Intercept — Best for Heavy Evening Use"}]
38:["$","p","17",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Gunnar's amber-tinted lenses block ~65% of blue light — the trade-off is that screens look subtly yellow. For dedicated evening use (gaming, late work sessions, scrolling before bed), the blocking is worth the color shift."}}]
39:["$","ul","18",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Blue light blocking:</strong> ~65% amber tint"}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Lens features:</strong> Anti-reflective, mild +0.20 diopter boost"}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Frame:</strong> Lightweight nylon"}}],["$","li","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Price:</strong> ~$80"}}]]}]
3a:["$","p","19",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Note: don't use these during creative color work. Photo editing, design, video — the tint will throw your color judgment off."}}]
3b:["$","h3","20",{"id":"3-warby-parker-with-blue-light-add-on-best-mainstream-option","children":"3. Warby Parker (with blue-light add-on) — Best Mainstream Option"}]
3c:["$","p","21",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Warby Parker isn't a blue-light specialist but lets you add blue-light blocking to any of their frames for a small upcharge. If you care more about how the glasses look than peak blocking percentage, this is the path."}}]
3d:["$","ul","22",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Blue light blocking:</strong> ~10–15% (clear lens)"}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Lens features:</strong> Anti-reflective standard, optional reading boost"}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Frame:</strong> 200+ frames in their catalog"}}],["$","li","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Price:</strong> Frames from $95, blue-light add-on +$50"}}]]}]
3e:["$","h3","23",{"id":"4-amazon-basics-blue-light-glasses-best-budget","children":"4. Amazon Basics Blue Light Glasses — Best Budget"}]
3f:["$","p","24",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"For testing whether blue light glasses make any difference for you, $20 unbranded glasses do the job. Don't expect premium build, but the blocking percentage is comparable to the $95 alternatives."}}]
40:["$","ul","25",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Blue light blocking:</strong> ~30% claimed"}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Lens features:</strong> Basic anti-reflective"}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Frame:</strong> Plastic, multiple shapes"}}],["$","li","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Price:</strong> ~$20"}}]]}]
41:["$","p","26",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Treat this as a \"test the concept\" purchase. If they help, upgrade to a Felix Gray."}}]
42:["$","h3","27",{"id":"5-eyebuydirect-blue-light-glasses-best-for-prescription-wearers","children":"5. Eyebuydirect Blue Light Glasses — Best for Prescription Wearers"}]
43:["$","p","28",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"If you already need prescription glasses, Eyebuydirect adds blue-light coating to any prescription order for $19. This is significantly cheaper than getting blue-light glasses from your eye doctor's office (often $150+)."}}]
44:["$","ul","29",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Blue light blocking:</strong> ~25%"}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Frame:</strong> Hundreds of options"}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Price:</strong> Frames from $30, with prescription + blue-light coating typically $80–$120 total"}}]]}]
45:["$","h2","30",{"id":"other-things-that-help-eye-strain-more-than-blue-light-glasses","children":"Other Things That Help Eye Strain (More Than Blue Light Glasses)"}]
46:["$","p","31",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"This is the honest section. If you have screen-induced eye strain, the highest-leverage fixes are:"}}]
47:["$","ul","32",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>The 20-20-20 rule</strong> — every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds."}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Monitor distance</strong> — at least an arm's length from your face."}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Monitor height</strong> — top of the screen at or just below eye level."}}],["$","li","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Ambient lighting</strong> — never use a screen in a dark room. Add a desk lamp or <a href=\"/blog/monitor-light-bar-vs-desk-lamp\" class=\"text-orange-600 dark:text-orange-400 hover:underline font-medium\">bias lighting</a> behind the monitor."}}],["$","li","4",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Artificial tears</strong> — staring at screens reduces blink rate by ~60%. Lubricating eye drops 2–3 times a day are remarkably effective."}}],["$","li","5",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Annual eye exam</strong> — most \"eye strain\" is uncorrected refractive error."}}]]}]
48:["$","p","33",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"If you've optimized all of the above and still have eye strain, <em>then</em> try blue-light glasses. They're the smallest lever, not the first."}}]
49:["$","h2","34",{"id":"frequently-asked-questions","children":"Frequently Asked Questions"}]
4a:["$","p","35",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Do blue light glasses really work?</strong>"}}]
4b:["$","p","36",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"For sleep quality (when worn 2–3 hours before bed), yes. For daytime eye-strain reduction, the evidence is weak. Most \"eye strain relief\" comes from the anti-reflective coating, not the blue-light filter."}}]
4c:["$","p","37",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Should I wear blue light glasses all day?</strong>"}}]
4d:["$","p","38",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Probably not. There's some emerging concern that all-day blocking of normal daylight blue exposure may disrupt circadian rhythm in the <em>wrong</em> direction. Save them for evening or after-dark screen time."}}]
4e:["$","p","39",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Can blue light glasses replace regular glasses?</strong>"}}]
4f:["$","p","40",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Only if they include your actual prescription. \"Computer glasses\" with a small +0.50 boost can help over-35s with mild presbyopia, but they're not a substitute for a real eye exam."}}]
50:["$","p","41",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Are screen filters or \"Night Shift\" mode just as good?</strong>"}}]
51:["$","p","42",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"For sleep purposes, software-based blue-light reduction (Night Shift on iOS/macOS, Night Light on Windows, f.lux) does roughly the same thing as glasses, for free. Use both for maximum effect."}}]
52:["$","p","43",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>What's the difference between $20 and $100 blue light glasses?</strong>"}}]
53:["$","p","44",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Frame quality, anti-reflective coating quality, and build durability — not blocking percentage. The $20 pair will block similar amounts of blue light. They'll just feel cheaper, scratch faster, and break sooner."}}]

5d:I[27201,["/_next/static/chunks/01xlw8hd842-c.js","/_next/static/chunks/0d3shmwh5_nmn.js"],"IconMark"]
20:null
25:[["$","title","0",{"children":"Best Blue Light Glasses for Computer Work in 2026 (Honest Review)"}],["$","meta","1",{"name":"description","content":"What blue light glasses actually do, what they don't, and the five pairs worth buying — written by someone who read the research instead of the marketing copy."}],["$","link","2",{"rel":"manifest","href":"/manifest.webmanifest","crossOrigin":"$undefined"}],["$","link","3",{"rel":"canonical","href":"https://deskbly.com/blog/best-blue-light-glasses-computer-work-2026"}],["$","meta","4",{"property":"og:title","content":"Best Blue Light Glasses for Computer Work in 2026 (Honest Review)"}],["$","meta","5",{"property":"og:description","content":"What blue light glasses actually do, what they don't, and the five pairs worth buying — written by someone who read the research instead of the marketing copy."}],["$","meta","6",{"property":"og:url","content":"https://deskbly.com/blog/best-blue-light-glasses-computer-work-2026"}],["$","meta","7",{"property":"og:image","content":"https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1499750310107-5fef28a66643?w=1200&h=800&fit=crop&auto=format&q=80"}],["$","meta","8",{"property":"og:image:width","content":"1200"}],["$","meta","9",{"property":"og:image:height","content":"800"}],["$","meta","10",{"property":"og:image:alt","content":"Best
